name: rling
title: rling

arguments.description: Use xxHash to hash each line. Saves memory
arguments.raw: -x		Use xxHash to hash each line. Saves memory

arguments.description: Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

arguments.description: Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

local_help.--help:
dedupe: unrecognized option '--help'
dedupe Version 1.5 2020/07/31 02:36:55 dlr Exp dlr $

dedupe [-u] [file file...]
If no files supplied, reads from stdin.  Always writes to stdout
If stdin is used as a filename, the actual stdin will read
	-x		Use xxHash to hash each line. Saves memory
	-S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
	-U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
			Specify a character like a,b,c, or a range like a-f,
			0x61-0x66 or as decimal values line 0-32.
local_help.-h:
dedupe Version 1.5 2020/07/31 02:36:55 dlr Exp dlr $

dedupe [-u] [file file...]
If no files supplied, reads from stdin.  Always writes to stdout
If stdin is used as a filename, the actual stdin will read
	-x		Use xxHash to hash each line. Saves memory
	-S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
	-U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
			Specify a character like a,b,c, or a range like a-f,
			0x61-0x66 or as decimal values line 0-32.

commands.name: getpass

arguments.description: Set extraction to N-, N-M, or -M like cut
arguments.raw: -c [colspec]	Set extraction to N-, N-M, or -M like cut

arguments.description: Set delimiter to character, decimal value, or 0x-style hex value
arguments.raw: -d [val]	Set delimiter to character, decimal value, or 0x-style hex value

arguments.description: Set extraction to field number. Starts at 1
arguments.raw: -f [field]	Set extraction to field number. Starts at 1

arguments.description: Disable extension exclusion entirely (equivalent to -x /dev/null)
arguments.raw: -n		Disable extension exclusion entirely (equivalent to -x /dev/null)

arguments.description: Disable extension expansion (file.txt -> file.txt.[hashtype], etc.)
arguments.raw: -t		Disable extension expansion (file.txt -> file.txt.[hashtype], etc.)

arguments.description: Read excluded extension list from file, replacing default
arguments.raw: -x [file]	Read excluded extension list from file, replacing default

arguments.description: This help
arguments.raw: -h		This help

arguments.description: Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

arguments.description: Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# getpass -h
 getpass Version 1.10 2023/09/02 05:27:23 dlr Exp dlr $
 
 extract passwords from result files
 
 	-c [colspec]	Set extraction to N-, N-M, or -M like cut
 	-d [val]	Set delimiter to character, decimal value, or 0x-style hex value
 	-f [field]	Set extraction to field number. Starts at 1
 	-n		Disable extension exclusion entirely (equivalent to -x /dev/null)
 	-t		Disable extension expansion (file.txt -> file.txt.[hashtype], etc.)
 	-x [file]	Read excluded extension list from file, replacing default
 	-h		This help
 	-S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
 	-U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
 			Specify a character like a,b,c, or a range like a-f,
 			0x61-0x66 or as decimal values line 0-32.
 
 Default excluded extensions:
 	.txt .orig .test .csalt.txt .fixme .new

local_help.man:
getpass(3)                  Library Functions Manual                 getpass(3)

NAME
     getpass - get a password

LIBRARY
     Standard C library (libc, -lc)

SYNOPSIS
     #include <unistd.h>

     [[deprecated]] char *getpass(const char *prompt);

 Feature Test Macro Requirements for glibc (see feature_test_macros(7)):

     getpass():
         Since glibc 2.2.2:
             _XOPEN_SOURCE && ! (_POSIX_C_SOURCE >= 200112L)
                 || /* glibc >= 2.19: */ _DEFAULT_SOURCE
                 || /* glibc <= 2.19: */ _BSD_SOURCE
         Before glibc 2.2.2:
             none

DESCRIPTION
     This  function  is  obsolete.   Do not use it.  See NOTES.  If you want to
     read input without terminal echoing enabled, see the  description  of  the
     ECHO flag in termios(3).

     The  getpass()  function  opens  /dev/tty (the controlling terminal of the
     process), outputs the string prompt, turns off  echoing,  reads  one  line
     (the "password"), restores the terminal state and closes /dev/tty again.

RETURN VALUE
     The  function  getpass()  returns  a pointer to a static buffer containing
     (the first PASS_MAX bytes of) the password without the  trailing  newline,
     terminated  by  a  null  byte ('\0').  This buffer may be overwritten by a
     following call.  On error, the terminal state is restored, errno is set to
     indicate the error, and NULL is returned.

ERRORS
     ENXIO  The process does not have a controlling terminal.

FILES
     /dev/tty

ATTRIBUTES
     For an explanation of the terms used in this section, see attributes(7).
     +---------------------------------------+---------------+----------------+
     | Interface                             | Attribute     | Value          |
     +---------------------------------------+---------------+----------------+
     | getpass()                             | Thread safety | MT-Unsafe term |
     +---------------------------------------+---------------+----------------+

STANDARDS
     None.

HISTORY
     Version 7 AT&T UNIX.  Present in SUSv2, but  marked  LEGACY.   Removed  in
     POSIX.1-2001.

NOTES
     You should use instead readpassphrase(3bsd), provided by libbsd.

     In  the  GNU  C  library implementation, if /dev/tty cannot be opened, the
     prompt is written to stderr and the password is read from stdin.  There is
     no limit on the length of the password.  Line editing is not disabled.

     According to SUSv2, the value of PASS_MAX must be defined in <limits.h> in
     case it is smaller  than  8,  and  can  in  any  case  be  obtained  using
     sysconf(_SC_PASS_MAX).   However, POSIX.2 withdraws the constants PASS_MAX
     and _SC_PASS_MAX, and the function getpass().  The glibc  version  accepts
     _SC_PASS_MAX and returns BUFSIZ (e.g., 8192).

BUGS
     The  calling process should zero the password as soon as possible to avoid
     leaving the cleartext password visible in the process's address space.

SEE ALSO
     crypt(3)

Linux man-pages 6.18               2026-02-08                        getpass(3)

commands.name: rling

arguments.description: Ignore any error/missing files on remove list
arguments.raw: -i		Ignore any error/missing files on remove list

arguments.description: Removes duplicate lines from input (on by default)
arguments.raw: -d		Removes duplicate lines from input (on by default)

arguments.description: Write duplicates to file
arguments.raw: -D file		Write duplicates to file

arguments.description: Do not remove duplicate lines from input
arguments.raw: -n		Do not remove duplicate lines from input

arguments.description: Output lines common to input and remove files
arguments.raw: -c		Output lines common to input and remove files

arguments.description: Sort output. Default is input order.
arguments.raw: -s		Sort output. Default is input order.

arguments.description: Number of threads to use
arguments.raw: -t number	Number of threads to use

arguments.description: Force size of hash table
arguments.raw: -p prime	Force size of hash table

arguments.description: Use binary search vs hash (slower, but less memory)
arguments.raw: -b		Use binary search vs hash (slower, but less memory)

arguments.description: Use rli2 mode - all files must be sorted. Low mem usage.
arguments.raw: -2		Use rli2 mode - all files must be sorted. Low mem usage.

arguments.description: Use files instead of memory (slower, but small memory)
arguments.raw: -f		Use files instead of memory (slower, but small memory)

arguments.description: Limit all matching to a specific length.
arguments.raw: -l [len]		Limit all matching to a specific length.

arguments.description: Maximum memory to use for -f mode
arguments.raw: -M memsize	Maximum memory to use for -f mode

arguments.description: Directory to store temp files in
arguments.raw: -T path		Directory to store temp files in

arguments.description: Do frequency analysis on input
arguments.raw: -q [cahwl]	Do frequency analysis on input

arguments.description: This help
arguments.raw: -h		This help

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# rling --help
 rling: unrecognized option '--help'
 rling version: 1.82 2026/03/22 17:24:36 dlr Exp dlr $
 
 rling - remove matching lines from a file
 rling input output [remfil1 remfile2 ...]
 
 	-i		Ignore any error/missing files on remove list
 	-d		Removes duplicate lines from input (on by default)
 	-D file		Write duplicates to file
 	-n		Do not remove duplicate lines from input
 	-c		Output lines common to input and remove files
 	-s		Sort output. Default is input order.
 			This will make the -b and -f options substantially faster
 	-t number	Number of threads to use
 	-p prime	Force size of hash table
 	-b		Use binary search vs hash (slower, but less memory)
 	-2		Use rli2 mode - all files must be sorted. Low mem usage.
 	-f		Use files instead of memory (slower, but small memory)
 	-l [len]		Limit all matching to a specific length.
 	-M memsize	Maximum memory to use for -f mode
 	-T path		Directory to store temp files in
 	-q [cahwl]	Do frequency analysis on input
 			a - all output, c - count, l - length, w - word,
 			s - running statistics, h - append histogram
 			Additional files will be matched against input files
 	-h		This help
 
 	stdin and stdout can be used in the place of any filename
 Total runtime 0.0013 seconds

examples.command: rling - remove matching lines from a file

Need at least an input and an output file to process.
rling version: 1.82 2026/03/22 17:24:36 dlr Exp dlr $

rling - remove matching lines from a file
rling input output [remfil1 remfile2 ...]

	-i		Ignore any error/missing files on remove list
	-d		Removes duplicate lines from input (on by default)
	-D file		Write duplicates to file
	-n		Do not remove duplicate lines from input
	-c		Output lines common to input and remove files
	-s		Sort output. Default is input order.
			This will make the -b and -f options substantially faster
	-t number	Number of threads to use
	-p prime	Force size of hash table
	-b		Use binary search vs hash (slower, but less memory)
	-2		Use rli2 mode - all files must be sorted. Low mem usage.
	-f		Use files instead of memory (slower, but small memory)
	-l [len]		Limit all matching to a specific length.
	-M memsize	Maximum memory to use for -f mode
	-T path		Directory to store temp files in
	-q [cahwl]	Do frequency analysis on input
			a - all output, c - count, l - length, w - word,
			s - running statistics, h - append histogram
			Additional files will be matched against input files
	-h		This help

commands.name: splitlen

arguments.description: Remove $HEX[] encoding from input
arguments.raw: -u		Remove $HEX[] encoding from input

arguments.description: Output to filename, modified with lengths
arguments.raw: -o filename	Output to filename, modified with lengths

arguments.description: Use char as place to insert number in -o file
arguments.raw: -c char		Use char as place to insert number in -o file

arguments.description: Sorts by length into a single output file.
arguments.raw: -s		Sorts by length into a single output file.

arguments.description: Sets the final buffer size for -s option
arguments.raw: -M size		Sets the final buffer size for -s option

arguments.description: Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

arguments.description: Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
arguments.raw: -U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# splitlen -h
 splitlen Version 1.10 2020/09/01 21:51:47 dlr Exp dlr $
 
 splitlen -o filename file [..file]
 	-u		Remove $HEX[] encoding from input
 	-o filename	Output to filename, modified with lengths
 			The char # will be replaced with _length in filename
 			If no # is in filename, _len will len appended to name
 	-c char		Use char as place to insert number in -o file
 	-s		Sorts by length into a single output file.
 	-M size		Sets the final buffer size for -s option
 	-S exp		Sets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
 	-U exp		Resets $HEX[] conversion for char or range
 			Specify a character like a,b,c, or a range like a-f,
 			0x61-0x66 or as decimal values line 0-32.

apt_metadata.description:
better rli
This package is similar to the rli utility found in hashcat-utils, but much,
much faster. rli compares a single file against another file(s) and removes
all duplicates.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
